stops.name: The Dubai Mall
stops.shortDescription: One of the world's largest shopping malls, featuring over 1,200 retail outlets. It also houses an Olympic-sized ice rink and a massive indoor aquarium.

stops.name: The Dubai Fountain
stops.shortDescription: The world's largest choreographed fountain system, set on the 30-acre artificial Burj Khalifa Lake. It shoots water up to 500 feet in the air accompanied by lights and music.

stops.name: Souk Al Bahar
stops.shortDescription: An Arabesque dining and lifestyle destination blending classic Middle Eastern architecture with modern luxury. The 'Sailor's Market' offers spectacular waterfront views.

stops.name: Burj Khalifa
stops.shortDescription: Standing at a staggering 828 meters, this is the tallest building in the world. Its design is inspired by the regional Hymenocallis desert flower.

stops.name: Dubai Opera
stops.shortDescription: A radiant center of culture and arts shaped like a traditional dhow boat. This multi-format venue can transform from a theater into a concert hall or a flat-floor event space.

stops.name: Burj Park
stops.shortDescription: A lush green island set against the imposing skyline of Downtown Dubai. It offers an unobstructed, peaceful vantage point of the towering Burj Khalifa above.

stops.name: Wings of Mexico Statue
stops.shortDescription: An iconic bronze sculpture created by Mexican artist Jorge Marín. Standing in front of the wings perfectly frames the Burj Khalifa in the background for a flawless photo.

stops.name: The Palace Downtown
stops.shortDescription: A luxurious, traditional Arabian-style hotel sitting right on the edge of the Burj Lake. Its grand, palm-lined entrance driveway is incredibly photogenic and serene.

stops.name: Sheikh Mohammed Bin Rashid Boulevard
stops.shortDescription: A wide, beautifully landscaped 3.5-kilometer circular boulevard that completely encompasses Downtown Dubai. It is lined with high-end cafes, restaurants, and public art installations.

stops.name: The Waterfall Divers
stops.shortDescription: An impressive 24-meter-tall indoor waterfall spanning all four floors of the Dubai Mall. It features stunning, life-sized fiberglass sculptures of human divers plunging into the water.

stops.name: Dubai Aquarium
stops.shortDescription: Located in the center of the mall, this massive suspended aquarium tank holds 10 million liters of water and houses thousands of aquatic animals, including rare sand tiger sharks.

stops.name: Fashion Avenue
stops.shortDescription: The ultra-luxurious extension of the Dubai Mall. It features its own five-star VIP services, gleaming marble walkways, and over 150 of the world's most prestigious luxury brands.

stops.name: Sky Views Observatory
stops.shortDescription: Located atop the Address Sky View hotel, this observatory features a thrilling glass slide and offers an unparalleled, elevated perspective looking directly back down over the Burj Khalifa.

stops.name: The Gate Building (DIFC)
stops.shortDescription: A short distance away, this monumental building is the centerpiece of the Financial Centre. Inspired by the Arc de Triomphe, it marks the transition into Dubai's thriving art and finance hub.
